audit_status.source_base: Greek Philippians text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for rejoicing, peace beyond mind, worthy thought, contentment, partnership/gift, final greetings.

verses.restored: So then, my beloved and longed-for siblings, my joy and crown, stand firm thus in the Lord, beloved.
I appeal to Euodia and I appeal to Syntyche to think the same in the Lord.
Yes, I ask you also, true yokefellow, help these women, who struggled together with me in the good news, with Clement also and the rest of my coworkers, whose names are in book of life.
Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, rejoice.
Let your gentleness/reasonableness be known to all humans. The Lord is near.
Be anxious about nothing, but in everything by prayer and petition with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to the Divine.
And the peace of the Divine, surpassing every mind, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Messiah Jesus.
Finally, siblings, whatever is true, whatever honorable, whatever righteous/just, whatever pure, whatever lovely, whatever well-spoken, if any excellence and if any praise, calculate these things.
What you also learned and received and heard and saw in me, practice these things; and the Divine of peace will be with you.
But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly that now at last you revived thinking on my behalf; indeed you were thinking, but lacked opportunity.
Not that I speak according to lack, for I learned to be self-sufficient/content in whatever I am.
I know how to be humbled, and I know how to abound; in each and every thing I have been initiated, both to be filled and to hunger, both to abound and to lack.
I have strength for all things in the One empowering me.
Nevertheless, you did beautifully/well sharing together with my pressure.
And you Philippians also know that in the beginning of the good news, when I went out from Macedonia, no assembly partnered with me in account of giving and receiving except you only,
because even in Thessalonica, more than once you sent into my need.
Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the fruit increasing into your account.
But I have received all and abound; I have been filled, having received from Epaphroditus the things from you, a fragrant aroma, acceptable sacrifice, pleasing to the Divine.
And my Divine will fill every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Messiah Jesus.
Now to our Divine and Father be glory into the ages of the ages. Amen.
Greet every holy one in Messiah Jesus. The siblings with me greet you.
All the holy ones greet you, especially those from Caesar's household.
The grace of the Lord Jesus Messiah be with your spirit.

translation_notes: Philippians 4:13 is contentment-under-conditions language, not triumphalist success formula.
Euodia and Syntyche are honored as women who struggled together with Paul in the good news; the appeal is reconciliation, not diminishment.
